blocks.content: The next morning was the first time in as long as Kit could remember that sitting next to Daisy in homeroom felt different.
Not bad. Just — aware. Like there was a new variable in a thing that hadn't had variables before.
They'd said hey to each other ten thousand times. This was the same word. It landed slightly differently.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Eli told me about the note thing.
The teacher came in. They both looked at the front of the room. Around them, homeroom did what homeroom did — the noise and the shuffling and the particular chaos of thirty kids trying to settle.
Daisy had her notebook open. Kit had his notebook closed. Normally she would have said something about that.
She didn't say anything about it.
